basic_details.job_title: Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026: Apply Offline for Direct Entry Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) Intake 06/2026
basic_details.total_vacancies: Direct Entry Havildar (Sports) and Naib Subedar (Sports)
basic_details.short_information: This overview table summarizes the key details of the Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 for Direct Entry Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts.

age_limit.minimum_age: 17½ years, completed on the first day of the enrolment period
age_limit.maximum_age: 25 years, not crossed on the last day of the enrolment period
age_limit.details: The age limit is the same for both Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts under this recruitment.

qualification.details: The minimum educational qualification for both Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts is Matriculation (Class 10th) from a recognized School/Board/University.

selection_process: The selection process for Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 involves shortlisting based on sports achievements
followed by trials and medical examination.

Applications are received only through Registered or normal post. Shortlisting is done based on higher sports achievements of the candidate. Call-up letters are sent to shortlisted candidates via email
time and place of selection trials. Candidates undergo Physical Fitness Test

and Skill Test at the designated Army Centre. Candidates qualifying the above tests undergo medical examination at the trial site itself. Candidates declared Medically Temporary Unfit may avail specialist review at a Military Hospital within 21 days; no further appeal is permissible. Candidates declared Medically Permanent Unfit may appeal for specialist opinion within 21 days on payment of Rs. 10 by Military Receivable Order (MRO); no further appeal is permissible. The final selection list is prepared from candidates who qualify in all respects
depending on available vacancies in each sports discipline. Selected candidates must submit a Police Verification form along with the Offer of Enrolment.
Selection is provisional until candidates report to the allotted Regimental/Corps Training Centre and complete their enrolment form.

physical_eligibility.1: Western Himalayan (J&K, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab Hill, Garhwal and Kumaon)
physical_eligibility.2: Eastern Himalayan (Sikkim, Nagaland, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Tripura, Mizoram, Meghalaya, Assam, Darjeeling)
physical_eligibility.3: Western Plains (Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Rajasthan, Western UP)
physical_eligibility.4: Eastern Plains (Eastern UP, Bihar, West Bengal, Jharkhand, Odisha)
physical_eligibility.5: Central Plains (MP, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Dadar Nagar Haveli, Daman & Diu)
physical_eligibility.6: Southern Plains (AP, Telangana, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Goa, Puducherry, A&N Islands)
physical_eligibility.weight: Proportionate to height and age as per Army Medical Standards
physical_eligibility.details: Candidates must meet the region-wise height, weight and chest expansion standards prescribed by the Indian Army for both male and female applicants. | Weight should be proportionate to height and age as per Army Medical Standards, and candidates should be capable of a chest expansion of 5 cms. | A height relaxation of 4 cms is given to female candidates belonging to the North East Region (Sikkim, Nagaland, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Tripura, Mizoram, Meghalaya, Assam), Gorkhas, Garhwalis, Ladakhis, and Tribals of recognized tribal areas. Domicile and Caste certificates will be used to determine applicability of this relaxation. Waivers in physical standards and tests may be given for exceptional talent, for both male and female candidates.

how_to_apply: Eligible unmarried male and female sportspersons must download, fill and post the application form as per the process below.
Download the application form from the official website www.joinindianarmy.nic.in.
Take a print of the application form on A4 size paper only, as per the prescribed format; non-standard forms will be rejected.
Fill in all details in block capitals, exactly as per the Matriculation certificate, including name, father's name, address, sports discipline, and level of participation.
Affix a recent colour photograph and provide left thumb impression and signature as indicated on the form.
Attach self-attested copies of Matriculation Certificate, Aadhar Card, and Sports Certificate(s).
Send the completed application, along with all required documents, through Registered Post only to the Army Sports Control Board address given below.
Ensure the application reaches the office before 1700 hours on 10 July 2026, as late applications will not be entertained.
important_instructions: Selection in the Indian Army is fair and transparent, based purely on merit; no bribe is required at any stage. Candidates must carry their Aadhaar Card for biometric authentication at the trial site. Entry into the Selection Trials site is permitted only on production of the Admit Card sent via [email protected]; fake admit cards will be handed over to Civil Police. Consumption or possession of Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances is a rejection criterion; zero tolerance is followed for alcoholism and substance abuse. Permanent body tattoos are permitted only on the inner forearm and back of the hand; offensive, obscene, or racist tattoos are prohibited. Sikh candidates whose religion prohibits cutting hair or shaving may retain hair, beard, and moustache as per laid-down specifications. No compensation is payable for death, injury, or loss during the trials; participation is voluntary, and an Indemnity Bond must be signed. Candidates may need to attend the Selection Trials site two to three times and must arrange their own stay. Use of contact lenses is not allowed during the trials. Certificates with overwriting, tampering of seals, or erasure/alteration will not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: This overview table summarizes the key details of the Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 for Direct Entry Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts.

rows.details: Indian Army / Army Sports Control Board, IHQ of MoD (Army)
Direct Entry Havildar (Sports) and Naib Subedar (Sports)
Unmarried Indian male and female sportspersons

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for the Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 should keep track of the following key dates.

content_sections.heading: Sports Disciplines Covered
content_sections.text: The Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 covers a wide range of sports disciplines, with specific events, positions, or weight categories notified for each. Sports achievements from 01 April 2024 onwards at International, Junior or Senior National Championship, Khelo India Games, Youth Games or Khelo India University Games will be considered for eligibility. Candidates with exceptional talent outside the listed events/positions may also apply. Women candidates who meet the qualifying requirements similar to male candidates are eligible to apply.

rows.specific_event_position_category: 100M, 200M, 110M Hurdle, 400M, 800M, 1500M, 20KM Race Walk, Decathlon, 400M Hurdle, 3000M S/C, High Jump, Triple Jump, Long Jump, Javelin Throw, Shot Put, Discus Throw, Hammer Throw, Pole Vault
Point Guard, Forward, Power Forward, Centre
Attacking Mid Fielder, Defensive Mid Fielder, Goal Keeper, Defender Centre Back, Defender Wing Back
Pivot, Right Back, Right Wing, Centre Back
Left Raider, All Rounder, Left & Right Corner, Left & Right Cover
400M/800M/1500M Freestyle (Long distance), 200M/400M Freestyle (Middle distance), 50M/100M Freestyle (Sprint), 50M/100M/200M Backstroke, Butterfly and Breaststroke
50M Rifle 3P, 10M Air Rifle, 10M Air Pistol, 25M Rapid Fire Pistol, Trap & Skeet

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must meet the marital status, nationality and sports achievement conditions specified for Direct Entry Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts. Only unmarried Indian male and female candidates are eligible for enrolment. Candidates discharged from the Indian Army for any reason are not eligible to appear in the selection trials. Candidates discharged from the Indian Air Force, Indian Navy, or any other Government organisation are eligible, subject to discharge with no adverse entries. Serving individuals must possess a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer at the time of selection trials. Medalist at Junior/Senior level representing a State in National Championship, or represented the Country at International level (individual event). Represented a State or Country in National or International competitions at Junior/Senior level (team event). Medalist in Khelo India Games, Khelo India Youth Games, or Khelo India University Games. Winner of any medal in World Championship/Asian Championships. Winner of any medal in Asian Games. Winner of any medal in CWG/World Cup. Represented India twice in Asian Games/Commonwealth Games/World Cup. Represented India in the Olympics.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The age limit is the same for both Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts under this recruitment.

rows.details: 17½ years, completed on the first day of the enrolment period
25 years, not crossed on the last day of the enrolment period

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: The minimum educational qualification for both Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts is Matriculation (Class 10th) from a recognized School/Board/University.

content_sections.heading: Physical Standards
content_sections.text: Candidates must meet the region-wise height, weight and chest expansion standards prescribed by the Indian Army for both male and female applicants. Weight should be proportionate to height and age as per Army Medical Standards, and candidates should be capable of a chest expansion of 5 cms. A height relaxation of 4 cms is given to female candidates belonging to the North East Region (Sikkim, Nagaland, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Tripura, Mizoram, Meghalaya, Assam), Gorkhas, Garhwalis, Ladakhis, and Tribals of recognized tribal areas. Domicile and Caste certificates will be used to determine applicability of this relaxation. Waivers in physical standards and tests may be given for exceptional talent, for both male and female candidates.

rows.region: Western Himalayan (J&K, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab Hill, Garhwal and Kumaon)
Eastern Himalayan (Sikkim, Nagaland, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Tripura, Mizoram, Meghalaya, Assam, Darjeeling)
Western Plains (Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Rajasthan, Western UP)
Eastern Plains (Eastern UP, Bihar, West Bengal, Jharkhand, Odisha)
Central Plains (MP, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Dadar Nagar Haveli, Daman & Diu)
Southern Plains (AP, Telangana, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Goa, Puducherry, A&N Islands)

rows.details: Proportionate to height and age as per Army Medical Standards

rows.standard: To be completed in 5 minutes 45 seconds

content_sections.heading: Pay, Allowances and Pension
content_sections.text: Selected candidates under this Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 receive the same pay, allowances, and post-retirement benefits as Regular Serving Personnel of the Indian Army. Pay and allowances as per the Army Pay Rules, 2017 and subsequent amendments. Pension governed by the Pension Regulations for the Army-2008, as amended from time to time. Other benefits including CSD facilities and medical facilities as applicable to Regular Serving Personnel. Insurance cover with premium deduction as per existing norms. Ex-Servicemen (ESM) status on release from service, along with ECHS and CSD facilities post-retirement. Promotion prospects exist on merit up to the rank of Subedar Major, with further opportunities for commissioned officer rank subject to qualifying examinations. The notification does not specify exact pay-scale or salary figures; candidates may refer to the Army Pay Rules, 2017 for detailed pay matrix applicable to Havildar and Naib Subedar ranks.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection process for Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 involves shortlisting based on sports achievements, followed by trials and medical examination. Applications are received only through Registered or normal post. Shortlisting is done based on higher sports achievements of the candidate. Call-up letters are sent to shortlisted candidates via email, indicating date, time and place of selection trials. Candidates undergo Physical Fitness Test, Physical Standards Test, and Skill Test at the designated Army Centre. Candidates qualifying the above tests undergo medical examination at the trial site itself. Candidates declared Medically Temporary Unfit may avail specialist review at a Military Hospital within 21 days; no further appeal is permissible. Candidates declared Medically Permanent Unfit may appeal for specialist opinion within 21 days on payment of Rs. 10 by Military Receivable Order (MRO); no further appeal is permissible. The final selection list is prepared from candidates who qualify in all respects, depending on available vacancies in each sports discipline. Selected candidates must submit a Police Verification form along with the Offer of Enrolment. Selection is provisional until candidates report to the allotted Regimental/Corps Training Centre and complete their enrolment form.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Candidates must attach certain documents with the application, and carry additional original documents at the time of selection trials. Self-attested copy of Matriculation Certificate Self-attested copy of Aadhar Card Self-attested copy of Sports Certificate(s) No variation in Date of Birth or spelling of name will be accepted between the application and certificates. Candidates will not be permitted to appear in trials without original educational marksheets and passing certificates, except where the school/college principal provides an original ink-signed certificate confirming the documents are deposited with the institution.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: Applicants should take care while filling and submitting the application to avoid rejection during scrutiny or at the trial site. Sending the application through speed post or by hand instead of registered post — both are rejected as per the notification. Submitting more than one application for the same intake, which leads to automatic disqualification. Mismatching name, father's name, mother's name, or date of birth between the application and the Matriculation certificate. Sharing personal email ID or mobile number with others, since all communication including call letters and results are sent only to the registered contact details. Reporting after 0600 hours on the trial date without valid justification, which forfeits participation. Bringing photocopied, computerized, or photoshopped photographs instead of original developed passport-size photographs. Carrying a mobile phone into the Selection Trials site, where it is strictly not permitted.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Eligible unmarried male and female sportspersons must download, fill and post the application form as per the process below. Download the application form from the official website www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. Take a print of the application form on A4 size paper only, as per the prescribed format; non-standard forms will be rejected. Fill in all details in block capitals, exactly as per the Matriculation certificate, including name, father's name, address, sports discipline, and level of participation. Affix a recent colour photograph and provide left thumb impression and signature as indicated on the form. Attach self-attested copies of Matriculation Certificate, Aadhar Card, and Sports Certificate(s). Send the completed application, along with all required documents, through Registered Post only to the Army Sports Control Board address given below. Ensure the application reaches the office before 1700 hours on 10 July 2026, as late applications will not be entertained.

rows.details: Directorate of PT & Sports, General Staff Branch, IHQ of MoD (Army), Room No 747 'A' Wing, Sena Bhawan, PO New Delhi-110011
Registered Post only (Speed Post not accepted)

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Selection in the Indian Army is fair and transparent, based purely on merit; no bribe is required at any stage. Candidates must carry their Aadhaar Card for biometric authentication at the trial site. Entry into the Selection Trials site is permitted only on production of the Admit Card sent via [email protected] ; fake admit cards will be handed over to Civil Police. Consumption or possession of Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances is a rejection criterion; zero tolerance is followed for alcoholism and substance abuse. Permanent body tattoos are permitted only on the inner forearm and back of the hand; offensive, obscene, or racist tattoos are prohibited. Sikh candidates whose religion prohibits cutting hair or shaving may retain hair, beard, and moustache as per laid-down specifications. No compensation is payable for death, injury, or loss during the trials; participation is voluntary, and an Indemnity Bond must be signed. Candidates may need to attend the Selection Trials site two to three times and must arrange their own stay. Use of contact lenses is not allowed during the trials. Certificates with overwriting, tampering of seals, or erasure/alteration will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q1. What is the Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: It is a recruitment drive by the Indian Army inviting unmarried male and female outstanding sportspersons for Direct Entry Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts under Intake Ser No 06/2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application is 10 July 2026, up to 1700 hours. Late applications will not be entertained.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 17½ and 25 years of age, with a date of birth between 30 September 2001 and 01 October 2008 (both dates inclusive).

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification required?
faq.answer: The minimum educational qualification for both Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports) posts is Matriculation.

faq.question: Q5. Can married candidates apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, only unmarried Indian male and female candidates are eligible for enrolment as Direct Entry Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports).

faq.question: Q6. What sports achievements are required for eligibility?
faq.answer: Candidates must have represented at International, Junior or Senior National Championship, Khelo India Games, Youth Games, or Khelo India University Games on or after 01 April 2024, with specific medal/participation criteria differing for Havildar and Naib Subedar posts.

faq.question: Q7. How should the application be submitted?
faq.answer: The filled application form, along with self-attested documents, must be sent only through Registered Post to the Army Sports Control Board address; speed post and hand delivery are not accepted.

faq.question: Q8. Is there an application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The notification does not mention any application fee for submitting the form.

faq.question: Q9. What physical fitness tests are conducted during trials?
faq.answer: Male candidates undergo a 1.6 Km run, 9 feet ditch, and zig-zag balance test; female candidates undergo a 1.6 Km run, 10 feet long jump, and 3 feet high jump test.

faq.question: Q10. Where can candidates download the application form?
faq.answer: The application form can be downloaded from the official website www.joinindianarmy.nic.in.
